subject: Mathematics
title: Counting Shortcuts
description: A short lesson to help you apply Counting Shortcuts.

hook.text: A restaurant offers 3 soups, 5 mains, and 4 desserts. How many meals can you build?

buildup.text: The multiplication principle: 3 times 5 times 4 = 60 possible meals.

discovery.text: This principle extends to passwords, license plates, and DNA sequences.

twist.text: A 4-digit phone PIN has 10,000 possibilities. An 8-character password has billions.

climax.text: Every time you choose from menus, the multiplication principle silently counts your options.

punchline.text: Multiply choices together and the options explode.

quiz.question: How many 4-digit PIN codes are possible using digits 0-9?
